steps.title: Identify what the evidence establishes
steps.content:
The passage proposes that increased atmospheric CO2 drove sauropod gigantism. The word "However" signals a direct contradiction: there is no evidence of significant CO2 spikes at any of the three key moments when sauropods evolved larger sizes. A proposed cause that is absent at every relevant moment cannot be the actual cause. The blank must complete a conclusion that follows from missing evidence for the CO2 hypothesis.

Now check each choice against that conclusion:
  • Choice A — 'fluctuations in atmospheric carbon dioxide affected different sauropod lineages differently.': The passage never describes fluctuations in CO2, only their absence at key moments. This conclusion has no support in the text. ✗
  • Choice B — 'the evolution of larger body sizes in sauropods did not depend on increased atmospheric carbon dioxide.': If CO2 did not spike at any moment when sauropods grew larger, then CO2 increases were not necessary for that growth. This is the direct logical conclusion the passage points toward. ✓
  • Choice C — 'atmospheric carbon dioxide was higher when the largest known sauropods lived than it was when the first sauropods appeared.': The passage says CO2 didn't spike at key evolutionary moments; it makes no comparison between CO2 levels at the start vs. the peak of sauropod size. ✗
  • Choice D — 'sauropods probably would not have evolved to such immense sizes if atmospheric carbon dioxide had been even slightly higher.': This reverses the causal direction. The passage discusses whether higher CO2 caused larger size, not whether higher CO2 would have prevented it. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The absence of CO2 spikes at every relevant evolutionary moment directly implies that sauropod gigantism did not depend on elevated CO2. That matches choice B — B is correct.
